Now I see the real picture — and a collision I have to flag. The shipped wiki already has its **own** `ADR-Index` with ADRs **0001-0004** (Hub/Node, Title/Copy split, etc.). **Our session's ADRs are also numbered 0001-0012 → they collide.** The 11 issues I just created reference "ADR 0001" etc., which now clashes with the repo's real ADR 0001.

I'll keep the wiki footprint minimal and honest: one epic page (not 12 duplicated ADRs), clearly namespaced, linked from the sidebar — without touching existing pages.

input.content:
# P2P / Self-Custody Re-architecture (Proposal Epic)

> **Status: proposal / migration epic — not shipped.** This documents a deliberate
> re-architecture of viewrr away from the shipped Hub/Node + Keycloak + central-serving
> design toward a Pear/Holepunch P2P + mnemonic self-custody design.
>
> ⚠️ **Numbering caution:** the ADRs referenced here (P2P-ADR 0001–0012) are an
> **independent set** living in `files(1)/docs/adr/` — they are **not** the shipped
> [ADR Index](ADR-Index) (`docs/adr/0001–0004`), despite overlapping numbers. Read the
> title, not the number.
>
> ⚠️ **ponytail note:** ~7 of these replace or duplicate systems that already ship
> (Hub/Node serving, Keycloak auth, egress-IP locality, central Title/Copy availability).
> This is a two-architecture migration, not additive work. Cost is real; see per-issue
> warnings.

**Milestone:** [#20 — P2P / Self-Custody Re-architecture](https://github.com/viewrr/viewrr/milestone/20)

## Decisions & issues

| P2P-ADR | Decision | Issue | vs shipped |
|--------|----------|-------|-----------|
| 0001 | Mnemonic Ed25519 self-custody identity | [#120](https://github.com/viewrr/viewrr/issues/120) | replaces Keycloak/OIDC (#112–115) |
| 0003 | Bare/Holepunch worklet + subprocess RPC | [#121](https://github.com/viewrr/viewrr/issues/121) | replaces Hub/Node HTTP core |
| 0001 | Self-custody clear-key playback; no DRM/license server | [#122](https://github.com/viewrr/viewrr/issues/122) | replaces server per-device keys (#111) |
| 0004 | Ownership model, not rental | [#123](https://github.com/viewrr/viewrr/issues/123) | policy (additive) |
| 0008 | Central catalog, pseudonymous DHT availability | [#124](https://github.com/viewrr/viewrr/issues/124) | swaps central Title/Copy who-has (#82–86) |
| 0009 | Peer selection: Plus Code + uplink | [#125](https://github.com/viewrr/viewrr/issues/125) | replaces egress-IP locality (#79) |
| 0006 | Secret-derived private topics + ephemeral pairing | [#126](https://github.com/viewrr/viewrr/issues/126) | new (Holepunch-only) |
| 0011 | Multi-device storage pool + encrypted backup | [#127](https://github.com/viewrr/viewrr/issues/127) | parallels central availability |
| 0010 | De-index-only moderation + TMDB allowlist | [#128](https://github.com/viewrr/viewrr/issues/128) | additive (cheap) |
| 0012 | No device fingerprinting (rejected) | [#129](https://github.com/viewrr/viewrr/issues/129) | a deletion (additive) |
| 0002/0007 | AV1+H.264 ladder; Compose Desktop; single crypto stack | [#130](https://github.com/viewrr/viewrr/issues/130) | reuse existing players; no new crypto lib |

## The additive subset (lazy keepers if the full migration is NOT taken)

0004 (ownership), 0010 (de-index moderation), 0012 (no fingerprinting), and "no Widevine
license server" from 0001 — these cost little and don't require the P2P rewrite.

## Full design record

12 P2P-ADRs + a context glossary live in `files(1)/docs/adr/` and `files(1)/CONTEXT.md`.

content.text:
Done. Shipped to `viewrr/viewrr`:

**Milestone #20** — "P2P / Self-Custody Re-architecture" (the epic)
**11 issues #120–#130** — one per session decision, framed as migrations off Hub/Node+Keycloak, each carrying its ponytail cost-warning, tagged `epic:p2p`.
**Wiki** — new [P2P Re-architecture](https://github.com/viewrr/viewrr/wiki/P2P-Re-architecture) page under a "Proposals" sidebar section. Existing pages (Home/Glossary/ADR-Index/subsystems) untouched.

**Two things you should know:**

1. **ADR-number collision.** The repo already has `docs/adr/0001–0004` (Hub/Node, Title/Copy split…). Our session ADRs are *also* 0001–0012. The issues say "ADR 0001" etc. — same numbers, different meaning. The wiki page namespaces ours as **P2P-ADR** and warns readers, but the 11 issue bodies still say bare "ADR 0001". Cheap fix if you want it: I rename our files to `p2p-0001…` and s/ADR 000X/P2P-ADR 000X/ in the issue bodies. Default: leave it, wiki disambiguates.

2. **The 12 ADRs + CONTEXT.md live locally** in `files (1)/docs/adr/` (inside the *tiny* repo), not in viewrr. The wiki links to `files(1)/…` which won't resolve for a reader. To put them in viewrr's `docs/adr/`, they must be renumbered first (else they overwrite the real 0001–0004).

Ponytail's verdict stands, recorded on every issue: ~7 of 11 rebuild or duplicate shipping systems — this epic is a real two-architecture migration, not additive.

Want the renumber/cleanup (kills the collision), or leave as-is with the wiki caveat?

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
DIR="/Users/jobinlawrance/Project/tiny/files (1)"
ADR="$DIR/docs/adr"

echo "== rename files =="
for f in "$ADR"/0*.md; do
  b=$(basename "$f")
  mv "$f" "$ADR/p2p-$b"
  echo "  p2p-$b"
done

echo "== fix path refs (docs/adr/000X -> docs/adr/p2p-000X) in ADRs + CONTEXT =="
for n in 0001 0002 0003 0004 0005 0006 0007 0008 0009 0010 0011 0012; do
  # path references
  sed -i '' "s|docs/adr/$n|docs/adr/p2p-$n|g" "$ADR"/p2p-*.md "$DIR/CONTEXT.md"
  # bare backtick cross-refs `000X` -> `P2P-ADR 000X`
  sed -i '' "s|\`$n\`|\`P2P-ADR $n\`|g" "$ADR"/p2p-*.md "$DIR/CONTEXT.md"
done

echo "== verify =="
ls "$ADR"
echo "--- sample cross-refs ---"
grep -h "P2P-ADR\|docs/adr/p2p" "$ADR/p2p-0008-central-catalog-mesh-contributed.md" | head -6

attachment.snippet:
1	# viewrr — Context Glossary
2	
3	Ubiquitous language for the viewrr P2P SVOD platform. Definitions only — no
4	implementation. When a term here conflicts with usage in a design doc, this file wins
5	(or the conflict gets resolved and this file updated).
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Identity
10	
11	The user's permanent cryptographic identity: an Ed25519 keypair. The `publicKey`
12	**is** the account (the "viewrr ID"). Derived solely from the **Recovery Phrase** —
13	never from the Master Password. The server never holds any part of the secret.
14	
15	## Recovery Phrase
16	
17	A BIP39 mnemonic (12 words default, 24 optional) that is the **sole root of trust**
18	for an Identity. `mnemonic → seed → DHT.keyPair` is the one and only identity
19	derivation. Entering the phrase on any device reproduces the exact same Identity.
20	Never transmitted, stored, or logged.
21	
22	*Resolved Q1: the mnemonic — not the Master Password — is the identity root. The
23	earlier `Argon2id(masterPassword)→keypair` derivation is retired; it would have made
24	recovery reconstruct a different keypair.*
25	
26	## Master Password
27	
28	A **local unlock secret only**. Encrypts the at-rest `secretKey` (and vaults) on a
29	single device. Changing it re-encrypts the local blob and does **not** change the
30	Identity. Not synced, not the identity seed.
31	
32	## Content Key
33	
34	A per-title 32-byte AES key. Sealed to the user's `publicKey` on entitlement, opened
35	with their `secretKey` inside the Bare worklet, then used to derive per-segment
36	AES-128 keys + IVs via HKDF. This is **self-custody clear-key** protection — decrypt
37	happens in app memory. There is no hardware DRM and no license server (see
38	`docs/adr/p2p-0001`). Anti-capture is limited to OS window flags (forensic watermark
39	deferred out of MVP), not cryptographic prevention.
40	
41	## Entitlement
42	
43	The **permanent** right to a title, embodied by a self-custody Content Key sealed to
44	the user's `publicKey`. Once acquired, a title is **owned forever** — the server
45	cannot revoke it (it holds no `secretKey`). Playback is offline and client-side. See
46	`docs/adr/p2p-0004`.
47	
48	## Subscription
49	
50	A gate on **what a user may newly acquire/download**, plus seeding perks and storage
51	tier. A Subscription does **not** revoke already-owned Entitlements. viewrr is an
52	ownership model, not enforceable rental — despite legacy "SVOD" wording in older docs.
53	(Subscriptions/payments are deferred out of MVP — see `docs/adr/p2p-0005`.)
54	
55	## NAS
56	
57	Your homelab node. Its viewrr role is **DHT bootstrap + Ktor metadata registry** only
58	(plus an optional *paid* backup tier later). It is **not** a content origin/seeder —
59	content originates from users' own devices. See `docs/adr/p2p-0005`. (Legacy docs calling
60	the NAS "origin seeder of all content variants" are superseded.)
61	
62	## Catalog
63	
64	The **central, searchable index** of titles available in the mesh, powered by ParadeDB
65	/ pg_search. Media files are decentralized (no central media server); the **catalog is
66	centralized**. Any peer acquiring a title contributes its metadata + availability, so
67	the catalog grows from mesh activity. Titles are keyed by **content UUID** (TMDB ID →
68	UUID v5) so the same film from different uploaders dedups to one entry. viewrr *is* the
69	index — this is the product's core SVOD differentiator, and its main legal exposure
70	(needs a takedown pipeline). See `docs/adr/p2p-0008` (supersedes the earlier "no catalog"
71	stance in `P2P-ADR 0005`).
72	
73	## Availability
74	
75	The fact that some peer holds a given title. Discovered **P2P via the DHT**
76	(`hash(contentUUID)` swarm), never from a central `publicKey ↔ title` table. All copies
77	of the same format are interchangeable sources — no copy is canonical. See `docs/adr/p2p-0008`.
78	
79	## Peer Selection
80	
81	Choosing *which* available peer to pull from: **nearest by Plus Code**, then **fastest
82	uplink**, with a fallback chain to the next-best peer. Entirely client-side. Governs
83	*where to pull*, not *what is authentic*. See `docs/adr/p2p-0009`.
84	
85	## Storage Pool
86	
87	The union of storage slices contributed by a user's devices — each device dedicates
88	**≥20% of free space**. The pool hosts and seeds that user's private vault and their
89	publicly-seeded content. There is no central media server; a user's own device pool is
90	the origin for their content. See `docs/adr/p2p-0011`.
91	
92	## Backup Tier
93	
94	An optional durability service: the NAS stores a user's **ciphertext-only** private
95	originals (no key, no plaintext, no backdoor). It is the single-device durability escape
96	hatch against total data loss. Ships functionally in MVP on jobin-nas; billing deferred
97	to phase-2. See `docs/adr/p2p-0011`.
98	
99	## De-index
100	
101	The operator's **only** moderation power: removing a title's row from the central
102	Catalog so it is no longer searchable. It does **not** delete the file — bytes remain
103	reachable via direct public link. No backdoor, no key escrow, no file deletion. See
104	`docs/adr/p2p-0010`.
105	
106	## Channel (phase 2)
107	
108	A creator-owned publishing space (SoundCloud/Dailymotion-style) for the creator's
109	**own rights-cleared media**. Channels are the paid layer, deferred to phase 2 — a
110	distinct opt-in publishing model layered on top of the neutral infrastructure base.
111	Not part of MVP.

2	
3	> **Status: proposal / migration epic — not shipped.** This documents a deliberate
4	> re-architecture of viewrr away from the shipped Hub/Node + Keycloak + central-serving
5	> design toward a Pear/Holepunch P2P + mnemonic self-custody design.

8	> **independent set** living in `files(1)/docs/adr/p2p-*.md` — they are **not** the shipped

10	> title, not the number.

13	> (Hub/Node serving, Keycloak auth, egress-IP locality, central Title/Copy availability).

15	> warnings.

Cleanup complete. Collision killed everywhere.

**Local** (`files (1)/`): 12 ADRs renamed `p2p-0001…p2p-0012`; internal cross-refs → `P2P-ADR 000X`; path refs → `docs/adr/p2p-*`; CONTEXT.md updated.

Now unambiguous: shipped `docs/adr/0001–0004` (Hub/Node) vs proposal `P2P-ADR 0001–0012` (`p2p-*` files). No number clashes anywhere.

**Full session output:**
- Milestone **#20** (epic) · 11 issues **#120–130** · wiki proposal page + sidebar link
- 12 `P2P-ADR`s + `CONTEXT.md` glossary in `files (1)/`
- 16 design forks grilled; ponytail flagged ~7 as rebuild/duplicate vs shipped — recorded on every issue.

One thing still true and worth a beat: the epic is a **real two-architecture migration** off the auth system (#112–115) actively being built. Issues are framed as such, but if this proceeds, those Keycloak issues need explicit close/redirect. Say the word and I'll link/close them against the epic.
